title: The number that is not the number
checkLabels.t7: You solved it by doing the maths
checkLabels.t12: The heading is still dark red
why.empty: The editor is empty. Hit the bin and start over.
why.untouched: The code is exactly as it was. The card still sticks out past the ruler.
hint2.untouched: One number in the .card rule. Nothing else changes.
why.unlinked: The page cannot find styles.css. You changed the link line.
hint2.unlinked: Hit the bin and start over.
why.used-inline: You used style in the tag. That is lesson 01 again.
why.touched-html: You edited the HTML. Here you work only in styles.css.
why.no-card: There is no element with the card class.
why.used-border-box: You used box-sizing. That switch exists and it does solve this — but not yet.
hint2.used-border-box: Here you are asked to work it out yourself. The tool arrives five lessons later and will mean more once you have seen what it fixes.
hint3.used-border-box: The arithmetic is not a punishment. It is how you remember what a box is made of — and you still need that after you flip the switch, because the border and the padding do not disappear, they are simply counted from the inside.
why.wrong-width: The card still does not end at 300 pixels.
hint2.wrong-width: Add up what the box takes: content, padding left and right, border left and right.
hint3.wrong-width: The padding is 20 on each side, the border 2 on each side. So there are 44 pixels around the content that width does not count. With width: 300 the box takes 344.
hint4.wrong-width: Subtract 44 from 300 and use the result as width. The padding and border stay as they are.
why.padding-cut: You deleted the padding. It is not spare — it is why the text does not touch the border.
hint2.padding-cut: The task is to calculate, not to remove. The padding stays 20.
why.border-cut: You deleted the border. It is part of the setup.
hint2.border-cut: The border stays 2. It goes into the sum, it does not come out of it.
why.ruler-moved: The ruler is no longer 300 pixels. It is the measure — it does not move.
hint2.ruler-moved: If the ruler moves, everything lines up and nothing is learned.
why.h1-broken: The heading is no longer dark red.
blocks.0.text: Open the preview. At the top there is a line exactly 300 pixels long. Below it is the opening-hours card — and the card says width: 300px. The two do not line up. The card visibly sticks out to the right.
blocks.1.text: The number that is not the number
blocks.2.text: Here is the rule for the card:
blocks.4.text: Three numbers. One is 300. The other two are 20 and 2 — and they do not fit inside the 300, they are added to it. The padding is 20 left and 20 right. The border is 2 left and 2 right. The box takes 300 plus 40 plus 4, which is 344 pixels. You wrote 300 and got 344, and nobody warned you.
blocks.6.text: Every element on the page is a rectangle of four nested layers, from the inside out:
blocks.7.items.0: Content — the text or the image. That is exactly what width measures.
blocks.7.items.1: Padding — the empty space INSIDE, between the content and the border.
blocks.7.items.2: Border — the line around the padding.
blocks.7.items.3: Margin — the empty space OUTSIDE, which pushes the neighbours away. It does not count towards the size of the box and has a lesson of its own.
blocks.8.text: width is not the width of the box. It is the width of the content.
blocks.9.text: So do I calculate every time
blocks.10.text: For now, yes. There is one line of CSS that changes this behaviour and makes width mean the whole box — nearly every project today puts it in the first few lines. It comes five lessons from here. That placement is deliberate: hand it to you now and you will switch it on and never learn what it switched off. And it does not remove the border or the padding — it only counts them from the inside.
blocks.12.text: Make the card end exactly at the ruler, that is, take up 300 pixels. The padding stays 20, the border stays 2, the ruler does not move. One number changes.
